title: Health Checks
description:
## Knowing When a Container Is Actually Ready

A running container is not the same as a *working* one — the process can be up while the app inside is still starting or wedged. A **health check** is a command Docker runs periodically inside the container to decide its health.

### In a Dockerfile

```dockerfile
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --retries=3 \
  CMD wget -qO- http://localhost/ || exit 1
```

### At run time

```bash
docker run -d --name web \
  --health-cmd='wget -qO- http://localhost/ || exit 1' \
  --health-interval=30s nginx:alpine
```

### The states

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `starting` | Within the grace period; not yet evaluated. |
| `healthy` | The last check exited `0`. |
| `unhealthy` | The check failed `--retries` times in a row. |

Inspect it with:

```bash
docker inspect -f '{{.State.Health.Status}}' web
```

Orchestrators (Compose, Swarm, Kubernetes-style probes) use this signal to delay traffic, restart, or fail a deployment. Mark complete, then add a health check to a container in the Advanced bundle.
